title: RV8GR bus ownership proof
purpose: Prove which device may drive IBUS and DBUS during each RV8GR phase so bus fights are visible before full-CPU testing.

chips.role: Bidirectional bridge between DBUS and IBUS
Accumulator output buffer onto IBUS for store
ROM/EEPROM DBUS read source when A15=0
RAM DBUS read/write target when A15=1

behavior.store_enable: /AC_BUF=NAND(T2,STR); LOW enables U14 and RAM write.
behavior.write_direction: WR_DIR=NOT(/AC_BUF); LOW reads DBUS to IBUS, HIGH writes IBUS to DBUS and disables ROM output.

ownership.T0: Memory drives DBUS, U7 drives IBUS from DBUS.
ownership.T1: Memory drives DBUS, U7 drives IBUS from DBUS.
ownership.T2_immediate: U34 drives IBUS from IRL; U7 and U14 are disabled.
ownership.T2_load_from_memory: Memory drives DBUS, U7 drives IBUS from DBUS.
ownership.T2_store: U14 drives IBUS from AC, U7 drives DBUS from IBUS, ROM output is disabled, RAM output is disabled by /WE LOW.

timing.control_window: Bus enables are intended to be stable inside each T0/T1/T2 phase before destination latches sample data.
unsafe_states: U34 and U14 both enabled on IBUS
U34 and U7 both driving IBUS
ROM output and U7 write both driving DBUS
RAM output and U7 write both driving DBUS

guarantees: T0/T1 fetch path has one IBUS driver through U7
T2 immediate has one IBUS driver through U34
T2 memory load has one IBUS driver through U7
T2 store uses U14 to IBUS and U7 to DBUS with ROM/RAM outputs disabled
ROM/RAM chip selects are complementary from A15
declared unsafe bus-fight states are detected by the proof model

audience: students ages 10-15, still useful for older learners
